purpose: Treasury reserve on Acropolis at start of war
notes: Pericles' pre-war accounting speech. This is the current coined silver reserve.

purpose: All-time high of Acropolis reserve before Potidaea and war expenditures
notes: Thucydides records this as the maximum the reserve ever reached.

purpose: Gold on Pheidias' statue of Athena, removable in emergency
notes: Pure gold ornaments on the statue, valued as emergency reserve.

purpose: Annual tribute revenue from allied states (Delian League)
notes: Pericles cites 600 talents as peak tribute; operational figure closer to 400 by 431.

purpose: Hoplites available for field service
notes: Excluding garrison troops and older reservists.

purpose: Garrison and reserve hoplites for defense of Attica and Piraeus
notes: Older men and metics serving in garrison duty.

purpose: Cavalry including mounted archers
notes: Total cavalry force including hippotoxotai (mounted archers).

purpose: Seaworthy triremes available for service
notes: Triremes in serviceable condition at start of war.

purpose: Guard ships kept on active patrol duty
notes: Ships kept continuously manned for harbor defense; significant ongoing cost.

purpose: Iron reserve: minimum inviolable treasury reserve
notes: Special decree reserving 1000 talents and 100 triremes for use only if fleet attacked.

purpose: Iron reserve: triremes reserved for emergency naval defense
notes: Companion to the 1000 talent iron reserve. Not to be used except in naval emergency.

purpose: Total expenditure on siege of Potidaea
notes: Potidaea surrenders after 2-3 year siege. Cost was enormous drain on reserves.

purpose: Corinthian fleet at Battle of Rhium
notes: Corinthian and allied fleet engaging Phormio's Athenian squadron.

purpose: Phormio's fleet at Battle of Rhium
notes: Phormio defeats larger Peloponnesian fleet using superior seamanship and diekplous.

purpose: Spartan fleet for second naval engagement at Naupactus
notes: Reinforced Peloponnesian fleet for rematch; still defeated by Phormio with 20 ships.

purpose: Athenian ships captured at Battle of Naupactus
notes: Athens lost only 1 trireme in second Battle of Naupactus despite initial setback.

purpose: Annual war expenditure estimate during Archidamian War
notes: Thucydides notes Athens was spending heavily; this figure inferred from tribute and reserve data.

purpose: Fleet deployed against Mytilene revolt
notes: 70 ships deployed to blockade Mytilene while simultaneously raiding Peloponnese.

purpose: Emergency property tax to fund Mytilene campaign
notes: First recorded eisphora (emergency property tax) of the war.

purpose: Cleruchies sent to Lesbos after Mytilene revolt suppressed
notes: 3000 lots divided on Lesbos; 300 kept sacred. Athenian settlers replaced Mytilenean landowners.

purpose: Annual tribute from Lesbos after revolt (per kleros)
notes: Each lot paying 2 talents; Athenian settlers rent the lots from the state.

purpose: Fleet to Sicily, also seizing Pylos
notes: Expedition to Sicily; Demosthenes persuades them to fortify Pylos en route.

purpose: Spartan fleet at Pylos/Sphacteria
notes: Spartan ships blockading Pylos before Athenian fleet's arrival.

purpose: Athenian fleet at Battle of Pylos
notes: Athenian fleet relieving Demosthenes at Pylos; defeats Spartan naval squadron.

purpose: Spartiates captured on Sphacteria
notes: Of 292 survivors, 120 were Spartiates. Enormously significant given Sparta's manpower shortage.

purpose: Artaxerxes' tribute demands, recorded in captured letter
notes: Letter from Artaxerxes captured; Thucydides notes he was 'not clearly stated' in his demands.

purpose: Brasidas' army in Thrace
notes: Brasidas marches north with 1700 hoplites (including helots and mercenaries).

purpose: One-year truce payment to Sparta
notes: Armistice of 423; no financial transfer recorded but tribute status of cities discussed.

purpose: Peace of Nicias: cities to pay tribute as before
notes: Peace terms restore tribute arrangements; actual figure from Athenian tribute lists ca. 421.

purpose: Quadruple alliance: Athenian hoplites committed
notes: Defensive alliance with Argos, Mantinea, Elis; Athens commits 1000 hoplites and 1000 cavalry.

purpose: Quadruple alliance: Athenian cavalry committed
notes: Along with 1000 hoplites, Athens commits 1000 cavalry to the alliance.

purpose: Spartan army at Battle of Mantinea
notes: Spartan force at Mantinea; Thucydides gives complex calculation suggesting ~9000 total.

purpose: Fleet sent to conquer Melos
notes: 38 Athenian ships, 3 Chian, 6 Lesbian; 3100 soldiers. Melos destroyed, men killed, women enslaved.

purpose: Initial Sicilian Expedition allocation
notes: Assembly votes 100 triremes and unlimited funds for Sicilian Expedition.

purpose: Drachmas per day pay for hoplites on Sicilian Expedition
notes: One drachma per day for hoplites; trierarchs received extra allowance for rowers.

purpose: Trireme cost to fit out with full crew for Sicilian Expedition
notes: Thucydides describes trierarchs spending privately on top of state pay; exact cost not given.

purpose: Athenian hoplites on Sicilian Expedition
notes: 5100 Athenian and allied hoplites; includes 1500 Athenian citizens.

purpose: Triremes on Sicilian Expedition (Athenian)
notes: 100 Athenian triremes, plus 34 Chian and Lesboan. Largest expedition Athens ever sent.

purpose: Reinforcements sent to Sicily under Demosthenes
notes: Second expedition: 73 ships, 5000 hoplites under Demosthenes and Eurymedon.

purpose: Spartan fortification of Decelea in Attica
notes: Permanent Spartan fort in Attica causes loss of 20,000 slaves and stops Laurium silver mining.

purpose: Slaves who deserted to Sparta after Decelea fortified
notes: Thucydides estimates 20,000 slaves deserted, mostly skilled craftsmen, devastating Athenian economy.

purpose: Replacement of tribute with 5% harbor tax after 413
notes: Athens replaces phoros (tribute) with 5% tax on all goods passing through allied ports.

purpose: Athenian ships lost in Sicily total
notes: Thucydides says losses were 'many' ships; 200 is the commonly accepted total including both expeditions.

purpose: Athenian troops lost in Sicilian Expedition
notes: Thucydides calls it the greatest defeat of any Greek force. ~40-50k men lost over two expeditions.

purpose: Emergency board of probouloi established after Sicily
notes: Athens sets up 10-man emergency council to manage crisis finances after Sicilian disaster.

purpose: Spartan fleet for Aegean operations after Sicily
notes: Sparta mobilizes 94 ships to exploit Athens' weakness after Sicilian disaster.

purpose: Tissaphernes' subsidy to Spartan fleet: monthly rate
notes: Tissaphernes pays Sparta 1 Attic drachma per man per day, then cuts to half drachma.

purpose: Pharnabazus' subsidy: full drachma maintained for northern fleet
notes: Pharnabazus maintains full pay unlike Tissaphernes; strategic competition for Spartan alliance.

purpose: Spartan-Persian treaty: Persia funds all Spartan ships in Asia
notes: Third Spartan-Persian treaty; Persia agrees to fund entire Spartan fleet in exchange for recognition of Persian authority over Asia.

purpose: Athenian fleet at Samos after oligarchic coup
notes: Democratic fleet at Samos refuses to recognize Four Hundred; maintains war effort independently.

purpose: Athenian triremes lost at Battle of Eretria
notes: Athens loses 22 ships at Eretria; Euboea revolts, cutting off crucial food supply.

purpose: Spartan assessment of their financial weakness
notes: Archidamus warns Spartans they lack money and naval skill; need time to build up resources.

purpose: Annual tribute at Delian League's founding
notes: Aristides sets the first assessment at 460 talents; the phoros that funded Athenian hegemony.

purpose: Commutation of military service to tribute payment
notes: Allies pay money instead of ships; Athens builds fleet with tribute, allies lose military capacity.

purpose: Cost of suppressing Samian revolt
notes: Isocrates and other sources suggest 1200-1400 talents; reflects costs of 9-month siege of Samos.

purpose: First Athenian invasion of Megara: hoplites
notes: Largest land force Athens sent in early war; accompanied by 3000 metics.

purpose: Fleet raiding Peloponnese during plague
notes: 100 ships sent; plague raging, losses to disease far exceeded combat losses.

purpose: Fine imposed on Pericles after his leadership questioned
notes: Pericles fined 15-50 talents (sources vary); Thucydides implies it was substantial.

purpose: Cleon's proposal: value of Mytilene to Athens annually
notes: Cleon argues tribute-paying allies are profitable; no specific figure given for Mytilene's contribution.

purpose: Athenian ships in abortive Sicilian campaign
notes: Athens recalls 60 ships from Sicily after generals negotiate unauthorized peace.
